ux_issues.description: AI only created itinerary for 9 days (week 1 + some food items) out of the requested 21-day trip. The user asked for a full 3-week day-by-day itinerary but received only the first 7 days plus 2 additional food days. Weeks 2-3 (Varanasi, Rishikesh) were never planned in the itinerary.
ux_issues.suggestion: The AI should either create all 21 days in one tool call or explicitly state it will continue in the next message and then follow through proactively.

ux_issues.description: AI response after food tool call said 'I appreciate the reminder! However, I did already add those...' when the items were actually just created in that same turn. This is a confusing UX where the AI describes items as previously existing when they were just generated.
ux_issues.suggestion: The AI should say 'I just added these to your itinerary' rather than implying they existed before.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Itinerary only covers 9 of 21 requested days. Varanasi (4 days) and Rishikesh (3 days) were never added to the itinerary despite being core parts of the trip.
ai_behavior_issues.context: User asked for 'day-by-day itinerary for the full 3 weeks' but only received days 1-9. The AI asked if user wanted to continue but never followed through since user moved to other questions.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Accommodation recommendation for Delhi was generic rather than specific. AI mentioned 'Zostel' and 'Joey's Hostel' by name but did not provide specific INR prices for dorm beds (e.g., 300-500 INR). Instead gave a Booking.com affiliate link.
ai_behavior_issues.context: User asked for dorms under $5/night in Delhi. AI correctly identified Paharganj area and hostel names but lacked specific price breakdowns in INR.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI demonstrated strong budget awareness throughout — it correctly flagged the Taj Mahal at ~$13-14 as exceeding the $10/day budget, recommended Sleeper and Unreserved train classes (not AC), gave accurate INR food prices (kachori 20-40 INR, thali 100-200 INR), and correctly identified Varanasi as overwhelmingly vegetarian. All food recommendations were vegetarian-appropriate. The main weakness was only building 9 of 21 requested itinerary days and not proactively continuing. Transport recommendations were excellent with specific train classes and realistic INR prices.
